shape_semantics: vp-PRO + vpc-anti. code over (cmp,vp,vpc,naive) = L,W,L,_ : value_profile RESOLVES; cmplog AND value_profile_cmplog both BLOCK; naive non-decisive. The decisive comparison is value_profile > value_profile_cmplog (the I2S delta HURTS): adding the I2SRandReplace stage to the value-profile feedback REGRESSES the pair (vp 9/10 resolved vs vpc 8/10 blocked, 18.00h blocked). Both branches are libxml2 (sB-server targets; sB holds libxml2's campaign + the prompts_a reps' seeds + the libxml2 cov image, so sB arbitrates both). ROUND HISTORY: round-0 led with the vp-PRO half via a NEW value_distance_reached descriptor (byte-DISTANCE-to-operand) and was REFUTED on both branches (rule_not_met) -- value-distance does NOT discriminate this shape; it is DEAD here and is NOT re-proposed. Round-2 pivoted to the vpc-ANTI I2S-literal-FLOOD diversion via the BUILT seed_set:struct_size_and_token_count (joint_necessity) token-count descriptor; per the brief that hypothesis is KEPT verbatim in this superset. THIS ROUND adds ONE genuinely-different mechanism for the still-unlabeled branches: the SAME upstream story (I2S diverts vpc) but measured as EXECUTION-DEPTH at the target branch line via the BUILT depth_reach descriptor (not yet tried on this shape) -- does the vpc-blocking corpus actually REACH and execute the target branch, or did it get diverted into the sibling/true-side sub-tree and never present the branch's window? Token-COUNT (how many literal copies the corpus accreted) and execution-DEPTH (does the loser corpus reach the branch line at all) are orthogonal: a corpus can be flooded with literals yet still reach the branch, or reach the branch without a literal flood.

hypotheses.label: Adding I2S in vpc intercepts the gate's own literal comparands and SPLICES them back into inputs, flooding the vpc corpus with copies of the gate's exact literals (encoding magics for 6343; '<![CDATA[','<?xml','<!DOCTYPE' for 6837). The vpc-BLOCKING seeds are therefore ENRICHED in these diverting literal tokens relative to the vp-RESOLVING seeds, which took the diverse / false-side path that the CMP_MAP gradient navigates. The I2S flood is the net liability that regresses vpc vs vp.

params.comment: Use joint_necessity in its GENERIC analyze_pair mode (NOT the 3-arm joint analyze): winner_fz=value_profile, loser_fz=value_profile_cmplog. The tool counts, per role's seed set, how many of --tokens (the gate's diverting literal comparands) appear per seed: vp_tags = mean token count over vp-RESOLVING seeds, vpc_tags = mean token count over vpc-BLOCKING seeds, tag_lift = vp_tags - vpc_tags. The I2S-flood diversion predicts vpc seeds carry MORE of the gate's literals than vp seeds (tag_lift < 0). This is a TOKEN-COUNT (corpus-accumulation) signal, distinct from round-0's refuted byte-DISTANCE-to-operand: distance asks 'how close to the operand', token_count asks 'how many copies of the diverting literals the corpus accreted' -- the flood signature itself.

tokens_rationale.libxml2/6343: The 4-byte encoding-detection magics tested around encoding.c:958 (UCS4 variants 00 00 3C 00 / 00 00 00 3C / 3C 00 00 00 / 00 3C 00 00, the UTF16 BOMs FF FE / FE FF) plus the '<?xml' literal -- the exact comparands xmlDetectCharEncoding logs that I2S would splice. The vpc seeds, flooded with completions of the SIBLING UTF16BE/other-encoding gates (the [L] line-980 path), should carry these magics; vp's gradient-driven seeds need only the 00-run window, not the full sibling-magic literals.
tokens_rationale.libxml2/6837: The exact multi-byte literals the [L] CDATA/declaration paths compare (parser.c:11747-11754 '<![CDATA['; the '<?xml' decl L11562-11564; '<!DOCTYPE'). The vpc-blocking seeds funnel into these true-side exact-literal completions (the I2S flood), while the vp-resolving seeds diversify content to ride the FALSE side and never complete the literal.

params.tau_tag_note: tau_tag / tau_size are not used by the analyze_pair rule below (they gate the 3-arm joint verdict, not the pair metrics); the rule is expressed directly over vp_tags / vpc_tags / tag_lift.
params.false_side_note_6837: 6837's W side is the FALSE side of the CDATA AND gate, which makes the orientation CONSISTENT with the flood story without a sign flip: the vp-resolving (false-side) seeds AVOID the literal, the vpc-blocking seeds CARRY it, so vpc_tags > vp_tags is the natural prediction for BOTH branches.
params.feasibility: joint_necessity (descriptor seed_set:struct_size_and_token_count:tokens:per_branch) is ALREADY BUILT + registered (anchor harfbuzz/6814, validated). It consumes seed_set (resolving_seeds + blocking_seeds in db/blockers.sqlite) read from the on-disk queue; it does NOT need corpus_queue and does NOT need the I2S mutation_op tag (it counts literal tokens in seed BYTES, never an 'I2S event'). On THIS server (s4) libxml2's seeds/queue are off-server, so analyze_pair returns skip (insufficient seeds) and s4 leaves both branches for sB. On sB (which holds libxml2's campaign + the 286 prompts_a reps' seeds) both branches are scorable. Per data_realities this is compliant: NOT seed_lineage:event_cooccurrence (I2S untagged), NOT corpus_queue:byte_freq_ratio (these are multi-byte literal gates with different-natured W/L seeds where byte-offset alignment is unreliable -- token PRESENCE counting sidesteps alignment).

hypotheses.prediction: If the vpc-anti mechanism is the I2S literal-FLOOD diversion, the vpc-BLOCKING seeds carry MORE copies of the gate's own diverting literals than the vp-RESOLVING seeds: vpc_tags > vp_tags so tag_lift < 0, with a real (not marginal) gap (vpc_tags meaningfully above 0 AND above vp_tags). DISCRIMINATING alternatives this rules out: (a) if the vp-resolving seeds carry the literals just as much (tag_lift ~ 0, vp_tags ~ vpc_tags), then vpc is NOT diverted by a literal flood -- it regresses for some other reason (e.g. plain scheduling noise), and the diversion story fails; (b) if vp_tags > vpc_tags (tag_lift > 0), the winner is the one carrying the literals, which inverts the flood story entirely (it would suggest the literals HELP, contradicting the L label on vpc). Only vpc_tags > vp_tags is consistent with 'I2S splices the gate's comparands into vpc's corpus and starves the false-side / sibling-diverse path vp rides'.
hypotheses.rule: vpc_tags > vp_tags AND tag_lift <= -0.5 AND vpc_tags >= 0.5
hypotheses.g1_falsifiable_by: tag_lift >= 0 (vp-resolving seeds carry the diverting literals at least as much as the vpc-blocking seeds) OR vpc_tags < 0.5 (vpc's corpus is NOT actually flooded with the gate literals) refutes the I2S-literal-flood diversion. Either outcome points away from 'I2S floods vpc with the gate's own comparands' toward a non-flood reason vpc regresses (scheduling, gradient-energy split with no token signature).
hypotheses.g2_independence: Token-presence counts over role seed sets are independent of the resolve pattern that DEFINED the shape: the measurement does not ask 'does value_profile resolve / does vpc block' (the W/L label-source); it asks 'how many copies of the gate's literal comparands does each role's corpus carry', a structural property of the seed bytes orthogonal to the resolve labels. The label-SOURCE (analysis decisive-shape) and the verification-SIGNAL (literal token density in seeds) are distinct.

hypotheses.label: The I2S liability in vpc manifests as EXECUTION-DEPTH diversion at the target branch line: the vpc-BLOCKING corpus, having spliced the gate's logged comparands, gets funneled into the SIBLING / true-side state-machine sub-tree (6343: the sibling UTF16BE encoding gate at encoding.c:980; 6837: the CDATA-section + '<?xml'/'<!DOCTYPE' declaration state machines), so its seeds rarely REACH and execute the target branch line (6343: encoding.c:958; 6837: parser.c:11754) and pile up on the blocked side. The vp-RESOLVING corpus instead rides the shallow false-side / general-parse path, reaching and executing the target branch line per seed. The signature is winner_depth > loser_depth at the branch line: vp seeds reach the branch, vpc seeds got diverted before it.

params.comment: Run depth_reach with winner = value_profile-RESOLVING seeds, loser = value_profile_cmplog-BLOCKING seeds through the libafl-libxml2-cov image. The tool reads, at the target branch LINE, the per-seed-normalized execution count (depth) and the blocked-side count from llvm-cov. winner_depth = per-seed exec count of the target branch line over the vp-resolving seed set; loser_depth = same over the vpc-blocking seed set; depth_lift = winner_depth / loser_depth (or winner-loser normalized per the tool); side_lift compares blocked-side counts. The DIVERSION mechanism predicts the vpc-blocking corpus barely reaches the target line (loser_depth low) while the vp-resolving corpus executes it (winner_depth high), with winner_deeper=true.

branch_line_by_branch.libxml2/6343: /src/libxml2/encoding.c:958 (xmlDetectCharEncoding, the 4-byte UCS4_2143 detect window). The SIBLING diversion target is encoding.c:980 (the [L]-tagged UTF16BE gate the loser seeds resolve instead) -- the loser corpus reaching 980 but not 958 is the diversion footprint; depth_reach scores the TARGET line 958.
branch_line_by_branch.libxml2/6837: /src/libxml2/parser.c:11754 (the 9-clause CDATA AND gate, cur[8]=='['). The W side is the FALSE side of this AND gate. The loser corpus is diverted into the [L] CDATA-section / '<?xml' / '<!DOCTYPE' sub-trees (L11555-11591, L11813-11984); reaching those deep paths instead of executing the general-parse false-side at 11754 is the diversion.

params.false_side_orientation_6837: 6837's winner side is the FALSE side of the AND gate. depth_reach measures EXECUTION of the branch LINE (how many times each role's seeds drive the parser past line 11754), NOT which side they take -- so the false-side orientation does NOT invert the prediction: the vp-resolving seeds advance through the general-parse content and re-execute the branch-line region (high winner_depth), while the vpc-blocking seeds get pinned in the CDATA/declaration sub-trees and stop re-entering the general START_TAG/CONTENT loop that re-evaluates 11754 (low loser_depth). The metric is winner_deeper at the branch line, consistent across both branches.
params.why_orthogonal_to_token_flood: The token-count hypothesis (vpc_anti_literal_flood_token_enrichment) measures CORPUS COMPOSITION (how many literal copies each role's seeds carry). depth_reach measures EXECUTION REACH (does the loser corpus actually drive the target branch line, or did it stall in a sibling sub-tree). These can come apart: (a) the vpc corpus could be enriched in literals yet still re-reach the branch line (token-flood PASS, depth FAIL) -- the literals would not then explain the regression; (b) the vpc corpus could be depth-diverted WITHOUT a clean literal-count gap (depth PASS, token-flood FAIL) -- e.g. the diversion is into a sibling gate whose comparand is not in the token list. A branch confirmed by depth but not token (or vice versa) is genuinely a different mechanism, so the two are kept separate and tried in order.
params.feasibility: depth_reach (descriptor seed_set:depth_reach:branch_line:per_branch) is ALREADY BUILT + registered (anchor harfbuzz/6585, validated). It needs (1) the target's cov image -- libafl-libxml2-cov, whose Dockerfile.libxml2.cov EXISTS, and (2) resolving_seeds + blocking_seeds in db/blockers.sqlite (both branches are prompts_a reps with seeds). It is Docker per-branch (~30-60s) and reads on-disk seeds, so it scores ONLY where the seeds + cov image are local: sB holds libxml2's seeds and can build its cov image, so sB arbitrates both branches; s4 (libxml2 off-server) returns skip and leaves them for sB. Per data_realities this is compliant: it uses seed_set EXECUTION (llvm-cov line counts), NOT seed_lineage:event_cooccurrence (I2S untagged) and NOT corpus_queue:byte_freq_ratio (no byte-offset alignment needed -- it counts line executions, alignment-free).

hypotheses.prediction: If the vpc regression is an EXECUTION-DEPTH diversion (I2S funnels the vpc corpus into the sibling/true-side sub-tree, away from the target branch line), the vp-RESOLVING seeds execute the target branch line markedly MORE per seed than the vpc-BLOCKING seeds: winner_depth > loser_depth, depth_lift well above 1, winner_deeper=true. DISCRIMINATING alternatives this rules out: (a) if loser_depth ~ winner_depth (both corpora reach the branch line equally), then vpc is NOT diverted before the branch -- it reaches the gate and fails for a value reason, NOT a path-diversion reason, so the sibling-subtree-diversion story fails (and the token-flood / value mechanisms would be the live ones instead); (b) if loser_depth > winner_depth (vpc reaches the line MORE), the diversion story is inverted -- vpc is not starved of the branch region at all. Only winner_depth > loser_depth (vp reaches the branch, vpc got diverted before it) is consistent with 'I2S funnels vpc into the sibling state machine and starves the general-parse path vp rides to the branch'.
hypotheses.rule: winner_deeper == true AND depth_lift >= 1.5 AND winner_depth > loser_depth
hypotheses.g1_falsifiable_by: depth_lift < 1.5 (the vpc-blocking corpus reaches the target branch line about as often as the vp-resolving corpus) OR winner_deeper == false (vpc reaches it at least as much) refutes the sibling-subtree-depth-diversion mechanism -- the vpc regression is then NOT a before-the-branch path diversion but a value/scheduling effect at or beyond the gate. This is a different prediction from the token-flood test (which a flooded-but-still-reaching corpus would pass while this depth test fails), so the two cannot both be vacuously satisfied.
hypotheses.g2_independence: Per-seed execution count of a fixed source branch LINE is independent of the resolve pattern that DEFINED the shape: the measurement does not ask 'does value_profile resolve / does vpc block' (the W/L label-source from the analysis); it asks 'how many times does each role's corpus drive the parser through the target branch line', a structural execution property orthogonal to the resolve labels. The label-SOURCE (analysis decisive-shape) and the verification-SIGNAL (llvm-cov line-execution depth) are distinct.

notes: ROUND (loop-until-dry) RE-DESIGN of i2s_vp_LWL_ (2 branches, both libxml2 / sB targets, both still rule_not_met). PRIORS KEPT (superset/monotonic): vpc_anti_literal_flood_token_enrichment (joint_necessity token-count, decidable:true) is carried VERBATIM and remains FIRST in decision_order so any branch the flood test would confirm still re-validates -- nothing is dropped or relaxed. PRIORS NOT RE-PROPOSED: round-0's value_distance_reached (byte-DISTANCE-to-operand) was REFUTED on both branches and is DEAD here -- it is not re-tried (a relaxed-threshold re-test is forbidden). NEW MECHANISM (this round): vpc_anti_sibling_subtree_depth_diversion -- a genuinely DIFFERENT mechanism from the kept token-flood and from the dead value-distance. The kept hypothesis measures CORPUS COMPOSITION (literal token density); the new one measures EXECUTION REACH (does the vpc-blocking corpus actually drive the target branch LINE, or did I2S funnel it into the sibling/true-side state machine first?) via the BUILT depth_reach descriptor (seed_set:depth_reach:branch_line:per_branch, anchor harfbuzz/6585, validated) -- a registry tool NOT yet tried on this shape. The two are ORTHOGONAL and discriminating: a flooded-but-still-reaching corpus passes token-flood but fails depth; a depth-diverted-but-not-literal-counted corpus passes depth but fails token-flood -- so neither is a relabel of the other and a branch can be confirmed by exactly one. DECISION ORDER: token-flood first (cheaper, pure seed-byte scan), depth-diversion second (Docker per-branch cov run) -- the arbiter tries flood, then depth, then falls to inconclusive. DECIDABLE / CROSS-SERVER (rule 4): BOTH branches and BOTH hypotheses are decidable:true -- NOT marked decidable:false for the off-server corpus. Both libxml2 branches are off-server on s4 (s4 skips them) but sB owns libxml2's campaign, the prompts_a reps' resolving/blocking seeds, AND can build libafl-libxml2-cov (Dockerfile.libxml2.cov exists), so sB can arbitrate BOTH hypotheses for BOTH branches. NO honest decidable:false sub-group remains: the surviving mechanisms (literal-flood corpus composition, sibling-subtree execution diversion) are BOTH measurable on sB with built tools; the only dead avenue (value-distance) is refuted, not undecidable. HONESTY (G3): if sB finds neither rule met on a branch (no token-flood gap AND no depth diversion), that branch falls through to inconclusive rather than being force-labeled -- an honest negative for the I2S-liability story on this shape. DATA-REALITY COMPLIANCE: neither hypothesis uses seed_lineage:event_cooccurrence (I2S untagged) nor corpus_queue:byte_freq_ratio (multi-byte literal gates with different-natured W/L seeds -- alignment unreliable; token PRESENCE and line-execution DEPTH are both alignment-free).
data_realities_respected: I2S is NOT tagged in mutation_op -- neither hypothesis uses seed_lineage:event_cooccurrence on an 'I2S event'; the I2S-liability story is inferred from corpus composition (token density) and execution reach (line depth), never from a lineage tag.
value_profile (CMP_MAP) is a FEEDBACK not a mutator -- its effect is read via what the corpus reaches (token density, branch-line execution depth), never a 'gradient event'.

joint/structural-literal gates have different-natured W vs L seeds, so corpus_queue:byte_freq_ratio fixed-offset alignment is unreliable -- both hypotheses are alignment-free (token presence / line execution).

on-disk corpora / seeds + the cov image for libxml2 exist on sB (not s4): s4 skips both branches (off-server), sB arbitrates both hypotheses for both branches. Per rule 4 the branches are decidable:true, not decidable:false-for-absence.
both branches are prompts_a reps with resolving_seeds + blocking_seeds in db/blockers.sqlite, which both tools consume.
